headline: Property Overhang Reaches 45,000 Empty Completed Units
context: Malaysia property overhang reached 45,000 unsold completed residential units in mixed developments valued at RM32 billion.

cards.big: 45,000 completed residential units worth RM32 billion remain unsold
cards.sub: Overhang concentrated in luxury mixed developments above RM500,000

cards.big: Johor accounts for 28% with 12,600 units primarily in Iskandar Malaysia
cards.sub: Forest City alone has 4,200 empty units visible from Singapore coast

cards.big: KL has 8,400 unsold serviced apartments averaging RM850,000 each
cards.sub: Developers continue launching despite existing oversupply in same locations

cards.big: Bank Negara tightened developer lending requiring 60% pre-sale before financing
cards.sub: Policy reduced new launches 25% but existing inventory continues aging unsold

cards.big: Property overhang tests whether development approval considers market saturation
45,000 empty completed units — the property overhang reflects a mismatch between what is built and what is needed.
